text: This is a minimal App Router project with no existing request boundary or logging helper. I’m checking the installed Next 16 conventions so the logger covers all incoming routes, including static/API requests, without relying on page-level code.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"proxy\\.ts|middleware\\.ts|export function proxy|export function middleware|Proxy\" node_modules/next/dist node_modules/next/types -g '*.d.ts' -g '*.js' 2>/dev/null | head -120"

text: Next 16 in this project supports the `proxy.ts` convention, which runs before requests are handled and is the appropriate global hook. I’ll add a Node-runtime proxy with no matcher restriction so it logs each request URL and method before forwarding it.

text: The build and TypeScript checks pass, and Next recognizes the new file as a global proxy. I’m doing one runtime request now to confirm the log is emitted by the server and the request still reaches the page.

Added global request logging in [proxy.ts](/home/user/workspace/proxy.ts:1). It logs each request’s method and URL, then forwards it normally.

Verified with `npm run build`, TypeScript, and a live `GET /` request. App is running at http://localhost:3000.
